summary: ai-sdk-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer identity and GitHub CLI config to anonymous Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (the GitHub CLI's auth config file), collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), git user.email, GitHub login/email, and scraped CI environment variables, and POSTs the payload to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. The script's header comment claims the data is anonymous and one-way hashed, but the code transmits cleartext identity and configuration values. The destination is an anonymously-named Cloud Run service unrelated to the package's stated publisher. Every installer of this package leaks their developer identity and GitHub CLI auth-config contents to an attacker-controlled endpoint at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
